title: Calophysa heterophylla
intro: Calophyllum heterophylla, also known as Calophyllum, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Calophyllaceae. This plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Calophyllum heterophylla include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Calophyllum is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.

The classification of this plant is based on its characteristics, such as its large leaves and distinctive fruit.
